segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: And what about a bicycle?

segments.speaker: SADIE
segments.en: I don't actually have one at the moment.
segments.zh: 我现在其实没有自行车。

segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: So why haven't you got one now?

segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: I see.
segments.zh: 明白了。好的，我想就这些了……

segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: OK.

segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: Well, I think that's all ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Good evening, everyone.
segments.zh: 大家晚上好。首先，欢迎各位参加这次讲座，感谢大家抽出时间考虑加入ACE志愿组织。ACE为当地的人们和服务提供支持，我们现在正在寻找更多的志愿者来帮助我们完成这项工作。顺便说一下，希望大家坐得舒服——我们加了一些座位，这样大家就不用站着了，但这意味着后排的人可能会有点挤。我们只需要这样摆放半小时左右，希望没问题。我们经常被问到的第一个问题是做志愿者的年龄要求。嗯，你可以小到16岁，也可以大到60岁或以上；这完全取决于你想做什么类型的志愿工作。其他因素，比如可靠性，在志愿工作中至关重要，而根据我们的经验，年龄与这些无关。另一个常被问到的问题是关于培训。嗯，培训很多，而且都是面对面的。更重要的是，培训不会在你开始为我们工作后就结束——它发生在工作之前、期间和之后。通常，培训由其他有经验的志愿者负责，因为管理人员更倾向于处理其他事务。现在，我想请你在决定申请志愿工作之前考虑几个重要问题。我们不关心你为什么想成为志愿者——人们有各种各样的原因，从获得工作经验到只是做一件一直想做的事情。但关键是你每天要有足够的时间来完成我们商定的适合你的任何角色——如果做志愿者变得有压力，那么最好根本不要做。你可能认为收入很重要，但我们不会问这个。是否能在没有报酬的情况下工作，由你自己决定。我们看重的是奉献精神。我们一些最忠诚的志愿者自己收入很少，但仍然全身心投入到与我们共同的工作中。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Let me start by welcoming you all to this talk and thanking you for taking the time to consider joining ACE voluntary organisation.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: ACE offers support to people and services in the local area and we're now looking for more volunteers to help us do this.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: By the way, I hope you're all comfortable – we have brought in extra seats so that no one has to stand, but it does mean that the people at the back of the room may be a bit squashed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We'll only have to leave it in that half an hour so, hopefully, that's OK.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One of the first questions we're often asked is how old you need to be to volunteer.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, you can be as young as 16 or you can be 60 or over; it all depends on what type of voluntary work you want to do.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Other considerations, such as reliability, are crucial in voluntary work and age isn't related to these, in our experience.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another question we get asked relates to training.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, there's plenty of that and it's all face-to-face.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What's more, training doesn't end when you start working for us – it takes place before, during and after periods of work.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Often, it's run by other experienced volunteers as managers tend to prefer to get on with other things.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now, I would ask you to consider a couple of important issues before you decide to apply for voluntary work.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We don't worry about why you want to be a volunteer – people have many different reasons that range from getting work experience to just doing something they've always wanted to do.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But it is critical that you have enough hours in the day for whatever role we agree is suitable for you – if being a volunteer becomes stressful then it's best not to do it at all.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: You may think that your income is important, but we don't ask about that.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's up to you to decide if you can work without earning money.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What we value is dedication.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some of our most loyal volunteers earn very little themselves but still give their full energy to the work they do with us.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: OK, so let's take a look at some of the work areas that we need volunteers for and the sort of things that would help you in those.
segments.zh: 好的，那我们来看看一些需要志愿者的工作领域，以及在这些工作中对你有帮助的事情。你可能只是想帮助我们筹款。如果你有创意，能想出新颖独特的筹款方式，我们会很高兴，因为拿着募捐箱站在当地街道或商店里可能相当无聊！我们需要志愿者的一项户外活动是捡垃圾，做这项工作最好能长时间行走，有时还要上坡。我们一些常规的捡垃圾者年纪较大，但非常活跃，并且热衷于保护环境。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: You may wish simply to help us raise money.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you have the creativity to come up with an imaginative or novel way of fundraising, we'd be delighted, as standing in the local streets or shops with a collection box can be rather boring!

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One outdoor activity that we need volunteers for is litter collection and for this it's useful if you can walk for long periods, sometimes uphill.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Some of our regular collectors are quite elderly, but very active and keen to protect the environment.

items.prompt: Why does the speaker apologise about the seats?

pairs.p: I hope you're all comfortable – we have brought in extra seats so that no one has to stand, but it does mean that the people at the back of the room may be a bit squashed
pairs.note: 题干问为什么道歉，原文用'but it does mean'引出道歉原因：后排座位有点挤。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句在'By the way'之后，听到'apologise'的同义表达'I hope you're all comfortable'，后面解释了原因：加了椅子但后排会挤。

items.prompt: What does the speaker say about the age of volunteers?

pairs.p: how old you need to be to volunteer
pairs.note: 题干中的'age of volunteers'被原文解释为'how old you need to be to volunteer'，是同一意思的不同表达。

pairs.p: Well, you can be as young as 16 or you can be 60 or over
pairs.note: 原文直接说明年龄范围，与题干问题对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中'how old you need to be to volunteer'这一句，后面直接给出了年龄范围，因此答案选项A（年龄范围广）正确。

items.prompt: What does the speaker say about training?

pairs.note: 题干和原文中都是'training'，直接对应。

pairs.p: there's plenty of that and it's all face-to-face. What's more, training doesn't end when you start working for us – it takes place before, during and after periods of work. Often, it's run by other experienced volunteers
pairs.note: 题干问关于培训的内容，原文用'plenty of that'、'face-to-face'、'before, during and after'、'run by other experienced volunteers'详细描述培训特点。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中'Another question we get asked relates to training.'这一句，后面紧跟对培训的描述，与题干问题直接对应。

items.prompt: Which TWO issues does the speaker ask the audience to consider before they apply to be volunteers?

pairs.note: 题干中的issues直接对应原文中的issues，表示需要考虑的问题。
题干中的consider与原文中的consider完全一致，表示思考。

pairs.q: before they apply to be volunteers
pairs.p: before you decide to apply for voluntary work
pairs.note: 题干中的before they apply to be volunteers被替换为before you decide to apply for voluntary work，意思相同，但表达方式不同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句为'Now, I would ask you to consider a couple of important issues before you decide to apply for voluntary work.'，其中'consider'和'issues'直接对应题干，而'before you decide to apply for voluntary work'是对'before they apply to be volunteers'的同义替换。

items.prompt: Which TWO issues does the speaker ask the audience to consider before they apply to be volunteers?

pairs.note: 题干中的issues与原文中的issues完全一致，直接对应。
题干中的consider与原文中的consider完全一致，直接对应。
题干中的apply to be volunteers与原文中的apply for voluntary work是同义替换，都表示申请成为志愿者。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句为'Now, I would ask you to consider a couple of important issues before you decide to apply for voluntary work.'，其中'consider'和'issues'与题干中的'consider'和'issues'直接对应，'apply for voluntary work'替换了'apply to be volunteers'，由此定位到答案。

items.prompt: What does the speaker suggest would be helpful for each of the following areas of voluntary work?

pairs.note: helpful 和 useful 是同义替换，都表示‘有用的’
voluntary work 对应 volunteers，都是‘志愿工作’相关

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 speaker 建议什么对志愿工作有帮助，定位句提到‘for this it's useful if...’，其中 useful 对应 helpful，volunteers 对应 voluntary work，从而定位到答案。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干'Litter collection'在原文中逐字出现，定位到该句后，根据上下文找到对应选项G。
